rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS KELLY INDIV AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, LARA KELLY MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to July 26, 2024 to be heard with the other pending motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS KELLY INDIV AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, LARA KELLY DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to July 26, 2024 for the Cross-Complainant to file a supplemental brief limited to citation to cases where a similarly worded indemnity clause (i.e. "all claims") was interpreted to apply to claims asserted by a party to the contract. If no such case can be located, the brief shall state so. There shall be no further argument or discussion of the cases cited. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DENA SUSSMAN-SCHECHTER VS. ADAM SKLARIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Per Ccp 664.6 And For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF DENA SUSSMAN-SCHECHTER , TRUSTEE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PER CCP 664.6 AND FOR JUDGMENT is GRANTED. This unlawful detainer is based on non-payment of rent. In the operative answer (February 7, 2024) defendant asserted an affirmative defense of breach of warranty of habitability. In the opposition to this motion defendants states that the habitability issues existed since 2022. Nevertheless, on March 4, 2024 Defendant entered into a stipulation agreeing to pay the full amount of rent/damages for the months of October to May 2024 in a lump sum by May 5, 2024. Defendant and his counsel admit that "no provisions regarding repairs were included in the Stipulation singed [by] the parties." (Fitzsimmons Declaration, paragraph 7). The parties further agreed "that this Stipulation shall be complete and final and shall not be subject to any claim of mistake of fact or law and this Stipulation is the full and complete agreement between and/or among them as related to this Unlawful Detainer action." All the issues relating to this unlawful detainer action have been resolved by this final stipulation. The payment, due on May 5, 2024 was not made and it does not appear from the opposition that Defendant is prepared to make the payment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
